title: 4 Establishing Policies for BPRP
body_text:
4	Establishing Policies for BPRP
4.1	Policy on Enrolling Business Members in BPRP
Your financial institution must establish policies for enrolling business members in BPRP to assist with risk mitigation and regulatory compliance. These policies can include, but are not limited to:
Applying best business practices such as âknow your customerâ
Ensuring that the business member is notified of their obligations under CPA Rule H6, Rules Pertaining to the Inter Financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement
Identifying Restricted Entities when processing applications from new billers
Informing the business member of payment posting and settlement timelines
4.2	Policy on Internal Controls and the Client Centre and FTP User Management
The section applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients.
Your financial institution must employ security practices and establish internal controls to monitor Client Centre and/or FTP Users. We recommend establishing internal controls and procedures to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner. Financial institution operations staff and external and internal auditors should pay particular attention to internal controls for managing Client Centre and FTP Users.
Policies can include, but are not limited to:
Ensuring Users have their own us IDs.
Important
:	Shared user IDs must be avoided.
Identifying and deactivating inactive Client Centre or FTP Users, or users who no longer require access to the system.
Prohibiting the use of multiple user IDs for an individual.
Ensuring that internal staff and business members who connect to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange via the Client Centre are advised of your financial institutionâs password policies.
See the CPS Admin Application Part, Section 3.3, Policy on Passwords, or the User Management Part, Section 3.3, Policy on Passwords, for more information on recommended password policies.
END
